PSTextureModes

The PSTextureModes field allows the texture mode for each of the four texture stages to be defined. This mode is ignored unless PSFinalCombinerConstants has the PS_GLOBALFLAGS_TEXMODE_ADJUST flag set. The texture modes most closely correspond to the tex* commands in the assembly language. It is most convenient to use the PS_TEXTUREMODES(t0,t1,t2,t3) macro to set the texture mode bits for each stage. The macro is defined as follows:

#define PS_TEXTUREMODES(t0,t1,t2,t3) (((t3)<<15)|((t2)<<10)|((t1)<<5)|(t0))

The following table lists the texture modes that can be set for each stage:

Texture Mode Stg 0 Stg 1 Stg 2 Stg 3
PS_TEXTUREMODES_NONE valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PROJECT2D valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PROJECT3D valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_CUBEMAP valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PASSTHRU valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_CLIPPLANE valid valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BUMPENVMAP   valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BUMPENVMAP_LUM   valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BRDF     valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_ST     valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_ZW     valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_DIFF     valid  
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_SPEC       valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_STR_3D       valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_STR_CUBE       valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DPNDNT_AR   valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DPNDNT_GB   valid valid valid
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT   valid valid  
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_SPEC_CONST       valid

The four texture address inputs at each stage are identified in the descriptions below as s, t, r, and q. Each of these inputs has single-precision floating-point resolution. In most cases, the output of each texture stage is a 32-bit value with four 8-bit components designated a, r, g, and b. Each of these components may be signed or unsigned. In some cases, this 32-bit value is interpreted differently. For example, the output of a PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT stage is a 32-bit floating-point number that represents the result of the dot product. Also, if the output of a texture stage is the input to a dot product, it may be interpreted as four 8-bit components or as two 16-bit components, depending on the dot mapping mode set for the stage doing the dot product.

The following texture modes may be specified:

PS_TEXTUREMODES_NONE
Indicates that no texture operations should be performed on this stage. It is important to set this mode on any texture stage that is not being used to ensure that no cycles will be wasted doing unnecessary texture lookups.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PROJECT2D
Performs a 2D projective texture lookup (argb = texture(r/q, s/q)). For a standard 2D texture lookup, q is typically 1.0 and the lookup reduces to (argb = texture(r, s)). This is the most common texture mode.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PROJECT3D
Performs a 3D projective texture lookup (argb = texture(r/q, s/q, t/q)). Again, when q = 1.0, this reduces to a simple volumetric texture lookup (argb = texture(r,s,t)).
PS_TEXTUREMODES_CUBEMAP
Performs a cube environment map texture lookup (argb = cubemap(r/q, s/q, t/q)).
PS_TEXTUREMODES_PASSTHRU
This is the same as texcoord. It does not do a texture lookup, so no texture should be set on this stage. The four inputs (r,s,t,q) are mapped to (r,g,b,a) outputs. This mode can be useful to debug a pixel shader as described in texcoord. In some cases, a vertex shader may wish to pass information to a pixel shader in addition to the diffuse and specular colors in v0 and v1. The information can be passed via the texture coordinates and then passed to the combiners via the pass-through texture mode.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_CLIPPLANE
This is the same as texkill. It eliminates the current fragment if any of the input texture coordinates meet the conditions set in the PSCompareMode register. By default, this register is set up so that if any coordinate is less than 0, the fragment is rejected. This mode is useful for implementing user-defined clip planes as described in the above section on the texkill instruction. No texture lookup is performed, so no texture should be set on this stage.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BUMPENVMAP
Identical to the texbem instruction. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BUMPENVMAP_LUM
Identical to the texbeml mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_BRDF
Identical to the texbrdf mode.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_ST
Performs the final stage of a 3 × 2 transform. It is identical to the texm3x2tex mode. The preceding stage must be set to the PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_ZW
Performs the final stage of a 3 × 2 transform. It is identical to the texm3x2depth mode. The preceding stage must be set to the PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_DIFF
Identical to the texm3x3diff mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_SPEC
Identical to the texm3x3vspec mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_STR_3D
Identical to the texm3x3tex mode. A 3D texture lookup is done with the dot product results. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product. This mode is similar to PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_STR_3D except it uses a cube map in the final stage instead of using a 3D texture.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_STR_CUBE
Identical to the texm3x3tex mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DPNDNT_AR
Identical to the texreg2ar mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use as inputs.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DPNDNT_GB
Identical to the texreg2bg mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use as inputs.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT
Identical to the texm3x2pad and texm3x3pad instructions. It calculates the dot product between the texture coordinates for this stage with the output register of the stage specified in the PSInputTexture field for this stage. The PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOTPRODUCT stage doesn't really operate independently. It must be part of a sequence of texture stages used to generate a 3 × 3 transform or a 3 × 2 transform as described above for the texm3x2pad and texm3x3pad instructions. The result of the dot product is used by a later stage.
PS_TEXTUREMODES_DOT_RFLCT_SPEC_CONST
Identical to the texm3x3spec mode. The PSInputTexture field specifies which texture stage results to use in the dot product.
Unpublished work. © 2000 Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.
